content: We can reframe Self-Refine, the existing no-training iterative self-feedback framework for output improvement, as a primitive single-perspective baseline, and extend it by integrating multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanism research to create a multi-perspective Self-Refine framework that still retains the core advantage of requiring no extra supervised data or reinforcement learning. This hybrid framework would address the key limitation of standard Self-Refine where repeated iteration from a single fixed perspective can get stuck in local feedback loops that fail to fix flawed initial outputs.
